narrative: Dear [Student's Name], imagine the admissions team looking at your application like piecing together a story about your journey to the bar exam, and unfortunately, the chapters they've seen so far paint a picture of real challenges ahead, leading to our tough decision to reject your application based on a predicted 51% chance of not passing. Your Year 3 decile of 3 stood out as the strongest signal, whispering that even midway through law school, your grades placed you in the lower middle of your peers, setting a pattern that often trips up students on the bar. This built on your Year 1 decile of 1, the second biggest factor, which showed an early struggle to find your footing academically right from the start. Your LSAT score of 24 added to that narrative, signaling to the model that the raw analytical skills needed for the bar's intense reasoning might be a stretch. Coming from a lower-middle family income bracket pulled things further in that direction, as it often means fewer resources for the extra tutoring or prep that can make a difference. Your Native American background, while a point of pride and resilience, has statistically been linked to higher hurdles in this high-stakes path, influencing the story toward caution. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.6 reinforced this, suggesting a consistent academic climb that's been steeper than most who succeed. Even as a full-time student and a woman pursuing this dream, those positive commitments weren't enough to outweigh the combined weight of these early indicators. It's like the model saw your profile as a mosaic where these pieces—led by those deciles—formed a riskier portrait than we can take on right now. We truly see your determination in targeting law school, but this prediction is about patterns from thousands of similar stories, urging us to protect your time and dreams from a likely tough outcome. If you strengthen those early academic footholds through targeted prep, your next chapter could rewrite this entirely. Keep pushing—you've got the heart for it.
